                                                                  CALENDAR OF FEATURES
                                            In addition to the monthly data appearing regularly in Employment and
                                            Earnings, special features appear in most of the issues, as shown below:

                                                                     Jan.   Feb.   Mar.   Apr.   May   June   July     Aug.   Oct.    Nov.   Dec.

                       Household data
                     Annual averages                                   X

                     Revised seasonally adjusted series                       x
                     Quarterly averages:
                      Seasonally adjusted data
                      Persons not in labor force
                      Persons of Spanish origin
                      Vietnam-Era veterans and nonveterans
                      Poverty-nonpoverty area data                     x                    x                   X              X


                       Establishment data
                     National annual averages:
                      Industry divisions (preliminary)                 X

                      Industry detail (final)                                        X

                     Women employment (National)                              X                   X                     x              X

                     National data adjusted to new benchmarks                                                                 (1)
                     Revised seasonally adjusted series                                                                       (1)
                    State and area annual averages                                                X

                     Area definitions                                                             x

                           The issue that introduces the establishment data adjusted to new benchmarks varies. The October 1975 issue marks
                    the introduction of March 1974 benchmarks.

                                             June 1976

             Labor turnover                                                      The "link relative" technique
                  Labor turnover is the gross movement of wage and salary            From a sample composed of establishments reporting for
             workers into and out of employed status with respect to             both the previous and current months, the ratio of current
             individual establishments. This movement, which relates to a        month employment to that of the previous month is computed.
             calendar month, is divided into two broad types: Accessions         This is called a link relative. The estimates of employment (all
             (new hires and rehires) and separations (terminations of employ-    employees, including production and nonproduction workers
             ment initiated by either employer or employee). Each type of        together) for the current month are obtained by multiplying the
             action is cumulated for a calendar month and expressed as a rate    estimates for the previous month by these "link relatives." In
             per 100 employees. The data relate to all employees, whether        addition, small bias correction factors are applied to selected
             full- or part-time, permanent or temporary, including executive,    employment estimates each month. The size of the bias
             office, sales, other salaried personnel, and production workers.    correction factors is determined from past experience. Other
             Transfers to another establishment of the company are included,     features of the general procedures are described in table F.
             beginning with January 1959.                                        Summary of methods for computing industry statistics on
                Accessions are the total number of permanent and temporary       employment, hours, earnings, and labor turnover.
             additions to the employment roll, including both new and
             rehired employees.
